item.title: Wall Street Seizes on Private Credit Fears With New Way to Short
item.description: Wall Street is debuting a new product that will give investors a way to wager against private credit.

item.title: Why Officials Are So Worried About Mythos, Anthropic’s New AI
item.description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell summoned Wall Street leaders to give them an urgent warning: an artificial intelligence tool from Anthropic PBC marks the beginning of a new era of cybersecurity.

item.title: Goldman Traders See Record Fast-Money Heading Into US Stocks
item.description: For equity bulls searching for the next catalyst to drive the market higher, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. traders have a message: watch for record buying from algo-driven funds.

item.title: Big US Banks Set to Temper Bond Sales After Strong 2026 Debut
item.description: The six largest US banks are expected to issue fewer bonds this quarter after the busiest start of a year on record.

item.title: Boaz Weinstein Scores Win After UK Trust Backs SpaceX Proposal
item.description: A Baillie Gifford & Co. trust that placed an early bet on SpaceX recommended a proposal backed by activist investor Boaz Weinstein after losing a key shareholder vote.

item.title: Senators Push CFTC to Probe Oil Trading Before Trump Iran News
item.description: Two Democratic US Senators pressed the federal derivatives regulator to investigate suspicious oil futures trading in the lead up to President Donald Trump making two major announcements about military operations in Iran.

item.title: Dallas Stars CEO Sees Rising Team Values on PE, Live Sports Boom
item.description: Private equity interest and the rising popularity of live sports are poised to extend an advance in National Hockey League team valuations after years of strong gains, said Dallas Stars Chief Executive Officer Brad Alberts.

item.title: In a Brutal Video-Game Industry, Two Indie Developers Are Teaming Up
item.description: The makers of the acclaimed Slay the Princess are funding the next game from the maker of the acclaimed 1000xResist

item.title: When Economists and Consumers Agree on Inflation
item.description: There’s no stripping out the effect that surging gasoline prices is having on the US cost of living.

item.title: Raízen Creditors to Demand Management Changes in Counteroffer
item.description: Raízen SA and its creditors are working on a new restructuring proposal for the company’s 65 billion reais ($12.5 billion) debt load following a week of high-stakes meetings in New York, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: France to Nearly Double Fiscal Support for Switch to Electric Power By 2030
item.description: French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said the government will divert investment to help households and businesses switch to electric power instead of handing out short-term fuel aid after oil prices surged on the Iran war.

item.title: The Inflationary Pressures Are Still Building
item.description: And consumer sentiment is plungin.

item.title: DeepSeek Looks for Data Center Engineers in Inner Mongolia
item.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is advertising two data center positions in Inner Mongolia, where the company reportedly is relying on banned Nvidia Corp.’s Blackwell chips.

item.title: Iran War Hits US Economy With More Inflation, Record-Low Sentiment
item.description: The war in Iran has fueled the biggest jump in US inflation in nearly four years and sent consumer sentiment tumbling to a record low. Economists say that’s just the beginning.

item.title: Trump’s AI Chip Export Push Stymied by Bureaucratic Bottleneck
item.description: President Donald Trump’s goal of significantly boosting global sales of American AI chips risks being undermined by licensing bottlenecks, staffing attrition and a lack of policy direction at the federal agency that oversees exports of billions of dollars in sensitive US technology.

item.title: Blackstone Eyes $2 Billion IPO for Data Center Acquisition Firm
item.description: Blackstone Inc. is considering an initial public offering that could raise $2 billion for an acquisition company that will snap up data centers, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Brazil Proposes Forming State-Run Rare Earths Firm in New Bill
item.description: A Brazilian lawmaker has proposed a bill to create a state-run company focused on rare earths and critical minerals in the latest push from the South American nation to localize refining and position itself as alternative to top producer China.

item.title: China Moves to Ban Sulfuric Acid Exports as Iran War Hits Supply
item.description: China has indicated it will halt exports of sulfuric acid from May, hitting metals and fertilizer industries already strained by raw material bottlenecks resulting from the Iran war.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Crypto Machine Stacks a New Layer of Leverage
item.description: The borrow-to-buy-Bitcoin model pioneered by Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. has spawned yet another web of startups tied to its fortunes — all of them exposed if Bitcoin falls.

item.title: SpaceX Ticker Speculation Is Heating Up as Tuttle Drops ‘SPCX’
item.description: A stock ticker that befits SpaceX ahead of its blockbuster IPO just became available.

item.title: Brazil, US Team Up to Tackle Cross-Border Organized Crime Gangs
item.description: Finance Minister Dario Durigan announced Friday that Brazil and the US have launched a joint-initiative to combat transnational organized crime gangs that have ramped up smuggling of weapons and illegal drugs across Latin America.

item.title: Iran War Spurs States to Consider Gas Tax Halt as Fuel Costs Surge
item.description: The war in Iran is prompting states across the country to consider temporarily cutting fuel taxes, a relief measure that could cost them millions of dollars.

item.title: Argentina Tweaks FX Rules After Cost of Getting Dollars Out Surges
item.description: Argentina’s government issued new rules for investors moving money out of the country after increased use of the parallel foreign-exchange market drove the cost of such trades to its highest in a year.

item.title: Vista Credit Sets Sights on Hard-Hit Software Loans Via New Fund
item.description: Vista Equity Partners’ credit-investing arm is raising $250 million for a new fund that will buy the beaten-down debt of software companies, seeking to capitalize on recent selloffs tied to concerns over artificial intelligence.

item.title: Surveillance Firm Hawkeye 360 Files for IPO in Defense Tech Boom
item.description: Hawkeye 360 Inc. filed for an initial public offering, joining a string of defense technology firms seeking to go public this year amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Switzerland Aims to Finalize US Tariff Accord by the End of July
item.description: Switzerland aims to finalize a US trade deal by the end of July, with the European nation pushing for assurances it won’t face higher duties than rival countries.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Drops to Record Low on Price Concerns
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in recent weeks to a record low, indicating Americans’ increasing worries about mounting inflation due to the Iran war.

item.title: Iceland to Cut Petrol Tax as Costlier Oil Risks Price Outlook
item.description: Iceland will temporarily lower taxes on gasoline to help ease inflation after the war in Iran sent prices of fossil fuels soaring.

item.title: As De Beers Bid Deadline Looms, Botswana Pushes for Control
item.description: Botswana is holding to its ambition of buying a controlling stake in De Beers from Anglo American Plc, potentially dampening interest from some private investors looking to acquire the diamond giant.

item.title: Here Are the Key Takeaways From the US CPI Report for March
item.description: Here are the key takeaways from the US consumer price index report for March, released Friday:
